element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First-person perspective navigation, learning layout of Orient Express carriages
Meeting ~30 characters with individual AI agendas; learning conversational outcomes affect story
Central driving force: uncovering why Tyler was killed, finding the Firebird treasure
In-game clock paces all NPC actions; player learns events occur at specific times; can rewind/fast-forward
Interact with objects in compartments and corridors for clues and inventory management
Uncover Serbian arms deals, Black Hand rebellion, international plots; escalates tension
Player must maintain false identity; risk of discovery increases as NPCs learn truth
Mechanical music box; critical MacGuffin connecting all character agendas and conspiracies

typical_relief_triggers: Finding safe alone time in own compartment
typical_tension_triggers: Real-time constraint of approaching journey destinations
Conflicting NPC agendas creating emergent confrontations
Multiple endings warning: wrong actions lead to arrest/death
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear, real-time event-driven; tension builds through convergence of NPC actions toward finale; player can manipulate pacing via time controls but cannot stop scripted story beats

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Knock on compartment doors; knock leads to NPC interaction or locked door, revealing agent availability and information
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Certain NPCs and items revealed only through investigation of specific compartments; overhearing conversations in corridors provides plot clues

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low friction; learn controls, train layout, meet NPCs; identity deception not yet challenged; murder mystery framing introduces story stakes
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate challenge; juggling multiple NPC agendas, time pressure increases, Anna discovers false identity, conspiracy depth escalates, must locate Firebird
difficulty_curve.late_game: High challenge; converging plot threads, Firebird ownership disputes, character deaths, approaching Constantinople destination; multiple endings create branching complexity
notable_spikes: Anna's confrontation about false identity (raises stakes)
Vassili's mental breakdown (emergency medical decision under pressure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player enters compartment, examines objects, talks to occupant, exits; each compartment is self-contained puzzle revealing character motive, agenda, or plot clue; creates rhythm of discovery-dialogue-departure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tyler's compartment (body discovery); August Schmidt's compartment (arms dealer); Kronos's private car (artifact collector)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player moves through train corridors and overhears NPC conversations; reveals information the player character cannot directly access; spatial positioning (near door) determines what is learned
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Overhearing Anna ask Tatiana to look after something (before interrupted by Vassili)

notable_level_patterns.description: NPCs follow timetable independent of player; specific events trigger at in-game clock times (e.g., character goes to restaurant car for lunch); player must anticipate and intercept or follow NPCs
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Characters' meal schedules, arrival/departure at stations (Paris, Strasbourg, Constantinople)

notable_level_patterns.description: As story progresses, more NPCs discover player is not Tyler; each discovery raises stakes; false identity becomes liability rather than tool; leads toward multiple endings (arrest, death, or alternate resolutions)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Anna's realization spreads to other characters; Miloš and Serbian associates become suspicious; Kronos may or may not care about deception
